headline: JetBlue's Winter Breakdown Resets Near-Term Risk--but Creates Long-Term Optionality

headline: Airline Stocks Drop on Disruption
summary: Airlines stocks slumped as the conflict in the Middle East disrupted operations across the region, crippling travel through some of the world's most crowded transit arteries. The escalation of the regional conflict shuttered airports, sparking one of the most drastic shocks to the industry since the Covid-19 pandemic, with the spillover affecting international carriers.

headline: Stock Index Futures Plunge Amid Middle East Conflict
summary: March S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESH26) are down -1.05%, and March Nasdaq 100 E-Mini futures (NQH26) are down -1.44% this morning as sentiment took a hit after the U.S. and Israel exchanged deadly strikes with Iran across the Middle East.

headline: Lockheed Martin stock rises on Iran conflict, Nvidia invests $2B
summary: Morning Brief host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Monday morning's trending tickers and stories. Defense stocks, like Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC), are surging on the latest military escalation in Iran. Meanwhile, the Iran conflict is causing airline stocks to plunge, including American (AAL), Delta (DAL), and United (UAL). Nvidia (NVDA) will announce $2 billion in Lumentum's (LITE) artificial intelligence (AI) research and development. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.

content.title: Lockheed Martin stock rises on Iran conflict, Nvidia invests $2B
content.description:
Morning Brief host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Monday morning's trending tickers and stories.

Defense stocks, like Lockheed Martin (LMT), RTX (RTX), and Northrop Grumman (NOC), are surging on the latest military escalation in Iran.

Meanwhile, the Iran conflict is causing airline stocks to plunge, including American (AAL), Delta (DAL), and United (UAL).

Nvidia (NVDA) will announce $2 billion in Lumentum's (LITE) artificial intelligence (AI) research and development.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.

headline: Airlines Pull Back from Middle Eastern Routes
summary: Major airlines are rushing to clear the skies above the Middle East.  As of Monday morning, more than 75% of flights in Israel, Qatar, Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates were scrapped, according to Cirium, an aviation data provider.  American Airlines, which partners with Qatar Airways to serve the region, has temporarily suspended its service to Doha.

content.summary: Major airlines are rushing to clear the skies above the Middle East. headline: Stocks to Watch Monday: Norwegian Cruise Line, United Airlines, Lumentum, Elevance
summary: ↘️ United Airlines (UAL), American Airlines (AAL): Global carriers extended cancellations and rerouted flights to avoid the Middle East. Airline stocks dropped. Meantime, regulators are looking to cut flights this summer at Chicago’s O’Hare airport, where United and American duke it out for supremacy.
